steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Jack's final line is sarcastic: he suggests Bartley would carry a 'leaky pail on your head in place of a hat, that way you'd not be without some cause of complaining.' The phrase always having a cause of complaining is the key signal of Jack's belief about Bartley.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Bartley will be able to help Jack with cutting the hay.': Nothing in the text suggests Bartley will help with the hay — he says he isn't at the fair for that purpose. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Bartley is concerned that Jack hasn't prepared for the weather.': Bartley complains about rain falling on himself, not about Jack's preparedness. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Bartley is a poor judge of the weather.': Jack doesn't question Bartley's weather prediction; he mocks Bartley's need to complain regardless of conditions. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Bartley will find fault with any situation.': Jack's sarcasm shows he believes Bartley always finds something to complain about — even if rain didn't come, Bartley would invent a reason. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
